channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Clean, Transform, and Load Data in Power BI: A Practical Guide to Data Preparation
item.description: INTRODUCTION Data cleaning is the process of identifying and correcting errors, anomalies and inconsistencies in raw data sets to improve the quality of the data and get it ready for advanced...

item.title: Ruby on Rails Has Scalability Issues. Here's How We Solved Them
item.description: Every few months someone declares Rails dead. Then Shopify reports another record Black Friday. In 2025, Shopify's Rails monolith handled 489 million requests per minute at peak with over 53...

item.title: Building Evenfield: An AI Homeschool Platform That Never Forgets
item.description: I built Evenfield because existing educational platforms treat every session like the first session. A student struggles with fractions in September, makes progress through October, then...

item.title: Shipping Better Dev Tools: What I Learned After Launching My React Generator CLI
item.description: Building Better Dev Tools: What I Learned After Launching My React Generator CLI A few weeks ago, I launched rgenex — a config-driven React architecture scaffolding CLI for teams. The goal was...

item.title: Privacy policy generator — free browser tool for developers
item.description: Introducing the Privacy Policy Generator As developers, we've all been there - scrambling to create a privacy policy for our SaaS projects, unsure if we're covering all the necessary bases. That's...

item.title: I Tried 10 ChatGPT Resume Prompts. Here's What Actually Got Me Interviews.
item.description: I sent 47 applications with my old resume. 0 callbacks. Then I tried 10 ChatGPT prompts I'd been collecting. I sent the next 12 applications with rewritten resumes. 4 callbacks. Here are the...

item.title: Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’
item.description: On Monday, the courtroom battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over alleged broken promises at OpenAI started, as usual, with jury selection. The only tricky part? A lot of the prospective...

item.title: A Unified View of AI Evolution: From Machine Learning to LLMs, RAG, and Fine-Tuning
item.description: Artificial Intelligence has progressed far beyond its early rule-based origins. What once depended on predefined logic has evolved into systems that can learn from data, reason through problems,...

item.title: ☯️ Stop Trying to Build the Perfect System. Do This Instead.
item.description: 🧘 The Final Lesson We’ve travelled a long road together. Article 1 – Every Software Architecture Is a Lie. Here’s Why That’s OK. Article 2 – How AWS Secretly Breaks the Laws of Software Physics...

item.title: I Joined Google Cloud NEXT ’26 Without Feeling Ready
item.description: A few days back, I came across Google Cloud NEXT '26 Writing Challenge via DEV Community. Initially, I thought: "Cloud computing? Seems like this is for the pros; not for me." However, something...

item.title: Claire's closes all 154 stores in UK and Ireland with loss of 1,300 jobs
item.description: Article URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cg4047qnpk2o Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47930150 Points: 9 # Comments: 0

item.title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk
item.description: Musk’s lawsuit challenges OpenAI’s evolution under Sam Altman. But during jury selection, several potential jurors voiced negative views of Musk himself.

item.title: Adaptive Ultrasound Imaging with Physics-Informed NV-Raw2Insights-US AI
Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube
item.description: Google is trying out an AI Mode-like search experience for YouTube. The company is now testing "a new way to search on YouTube that feels more like a conversation," with results pulling in things...

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
item.description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly...

item.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
item.description: If you're one of millions using element-data, it's time to check for compromise.

item.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
item.description: One of the most popular Linux distributions is about to get an influx of AI features. As reported by Phoronix, Jon Seager, VP of engineering at Ubuntu developer Canonical, shared a blog post on...

item.title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
item.description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

item.title: Trump demands ABC fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling for Disney to fire Jimmy Kimmel. On Thursday, Kimmel joked that Melania Trump had looked like an "expectant widow" in a skit about the upcoming White House...

item.title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
item.description: Over 600 Google employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding that Google block the Pentagon from using its AI models for classified purposes, reports the The Washington Post. Its...

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Conspiracy theories are swirling about the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: On Saturday evening, a room full of journalists, media personalities, and senior members of Donald Trump's administration descended into chaos as gunshots rang out at the White House...

item.title: Why the Steam Controller is (and isn’t) a big deal
item.description: Most PC gamers already have a controller they love using with Steam - a Sony DualSense, a 8BitDo Ultimate, a Nintendo Switch Pro, or something else. Part of that love comes from Steam treating...

item.title: Valve’s new Steam Controller isn’t perfect, but I’m buying one anyway
item.description: After my brief hands-on last year with Valve's new Steam Controller, I said it might be my dream controller. I've been looking for a controller with the customization and sheer function available...

item.title: Valve launches the Steam Controller without the Steam Machine
item.description: Last November, Valve introduced the world to its new vision of living room gaming: the Steam Machine and Steam Controller. Then, RAMageddon. Memory shortages forced Valve to delay all its hardware...

item.title: The missing step between hype and profit
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In February, I picked up a flyer at an anti-AI march in...

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web....

item.title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
item.description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”
